page: Microsoft Azure FX16-4ms_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the memory-intensive FXmsv2 family. It is powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The hardware configuration features 4 vCPUs, which are derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled at 2 threads per core. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache, alongside advanced instruction support such as AVX-512 and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for vector and matrix operations.

A defining characteristic of this server is its substantial memory capacity, providing 336.0 GB of RAM, which equates to 84.0 GB of memory per physical core. This high memory-to-vCPU ratio positions the instance for memory-bound workloads. The server does not include local storage, but it is premium storage capable, allowing external block storage attachments. It does not bundle any complimentary public IPv4 addresses and operates under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark data highlights strong single-threaded performance. The server achieved a Geekbench single-core score of 2278.0 and a text processing score of 2788.0, both placing in the top 10% tier. Single-threaded gzip compression reached 34.66 GB/sec, and bzip3 decompression reached 14.28 GB/sec, both ranking in the top 25%. Conversely, multi-core performance is more limited due to the low core count, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 4437.25 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression speed of 28.41 GB/sec, both falling into the bottom 25%. Memory read-only bandwidth for large blocks was also relatively weak at 35.88 GB/sec, while small block cached reads performed at an average of 345.21 GB/sec.

Given the combination of high single-core performance, massive memory capacity, and low core count, this server is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that require large memory footprints but do not scale across many parallel threads. It is well-suited for memory-intensive database operations, caching layers like Redis, which achieved 6.3 million SET operations per second in benchmarks, and single-threaded data processing tasks. It is less suitable for highly parallelized multi-core compute tasks or large-scale LLM inference, as demonstrated by a poor prompt processing speed of 4.05 tokens/sec on a 70B model.
description: The Microsoft Azure FX16-4ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server from the FXmsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs (2 physical cores) clocked at 4.2 GHz, supported by a large 260 MB L3 cache. The server is equipped with an exceptionally high memory capacity of 336.0 GB (84.0 GB per core) and has no local storage, though it is premium storage capable. Benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2278.0 and strong single-threaded compression speeds. However, multi-core performance is limited due to the low core count. This configuration is highly suited for memory-intensive, single-threaded workloads, in-memory databases, and caching applications like Redis, where massive RAM is required without the overhead of high vCPU counts.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX16-4ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 336.0 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-core performance, making it ideal for memory-intensive database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX16-4ms_v2 is a memory-intensive server with 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 336.0 GB of RAM, and strong single-core performance.
tagline: A memory-intensive virtual server pairing a massive 336 GB RAM allocation with high-frequency single-core Intel Xeon processing power.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs clocked at 4.2 GHz
Massive 336.0 GB RAM capacity providing 84.0 GB per core
Top-tier single-core and text processing benchmark performance
Premium storage capable with no local disk storage
Optimized for memory-intensive, low-concurrency database workloads
